item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The creepiness of the premise is OK, but the HHGttG refs ruin my suspension of disbelief. The jokey atmosphere overall seems much more like a -J than a main-series entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I was about to upvote this article for its very well-written containment procedure and description. i really like its effects and backstory, and the horrible implications about the other side of that cosmic portal. I'm a Buddhist myself, and animals suddenly talking in Pali creeps me out.

But then came the interview log. They're a big fan of Beastie Boys but they "screwed up" by mixing their songs up with Beach Boys'? The husband "accidentaly dropped" his iPod? Sounds too stupid and shoehorned in. Killed all the vibes. Upvote cancelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small nitpick, but Pyrex is a brand name. It would be more correct to use the generic "borosilicate glass," unless the Pyrex brand itself is somehow relevant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Headcanon: the ghost are Brian Wilson's ambition that was killed by the failure of SMiLe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny and weird, just the way I like it! Also, love the Hitchhiker's Guide reference!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God-dammit, ARD.

I had a whole idea for a Kanye West skip and you just… I need to stop it now, I need to drop it now.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Corrosive entities sure beats Minions Beach Boys. Or does it? On one hand, entities can kill. On the other, Banana. I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeezus Christ

English is not my first language and I actually screamed out what the f*** after reading this
-2nd time since SCP-1247 LaBeaouf viewer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And that's an upvote.

item.title: Re: "Minor" Task Force?
item.content:encoded:
Isn't that just Site Security?

I don't see the organizational value of calling something a minor task force. Having two different meanings of "MTF" in the same organization seems like a major source of potential confusion.

item.title: Re: "Minor" Task Force?
item.content:encoded: It's called a "Minor Task Force" because it has only one job (containing SCP-2570) and is composed of very few members for this reason, unlike full-blown Mobile Task Forces that would need to track down, capture, and then maintain containment of numerous SCPs, often simultaneously.

item.title: Re: "Minor" Task Force?
item.content:encoded: Regardless of how MTFs are organized, "Minor Task Force" seems like an odd and unlikely designation.

item.title: Re: "Minor" Task Force?
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't be opposed to that idea, actually. The idea of a MTF with several platoons, that can be called to unite and tackle a greater opposing force. Might make a cool storyline, reading about several specialists on something like a rescue mission, working in cojunction.

item.title: "Minor" Task Force?
item.content:encoded: Should it be "Mobile"? I am not sure what a "Minor Task Force" would even be. If it is supposed to be a smaller element of a larger force, then "nth squad of y platoon of MTF-x" makes more sense to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now, as the Kanye connection reminds me to much of the Tupac ghost one. It's making now suspect a Jay-Z and/or a Snoop Dogg SCP is coming soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *croissants

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Laws that, among other things, forbid these lesser Gods from intruding upon the territory of even more terrible and powerful Gods.

He's telling all those lesser Gods to hurry up with His damn massage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I didn't take it that way at all. Nowhere in the article is there a negative description of Kanye, the clueless fan/would-be-necromancer even having a playlist for him. (Edit: Ok, so there is one criticism, and that is that Asheem, the corrosive mass-possessing evil ghost doesn't like him.) It even specified that music including his voice works, rather than specifying his production-style.

So, I took it more as "Kanye is capable of repelling world-destroying, openly malevolent interdimensional spirits," which is pretty badass. I like to imagine that procedure Aleph-19(?) involves the MTF tag-team freestyling at the portal over 808s.

(Also, in the early draft, the music was just Beastie Boys)

I think if anyone's getting the shit end of the stick here, it's the Beach Boys. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A lot of people are assigning more significance to Kanye's use in the article than it actually deserves. The focus of the article is neither Kanye West nor his music, though they play integral roles; it is the portal above MCA. This article is simply a tribute to MCA, because I still greatly enjoy the music of the Beastie Boys.

Kanye was nothing more than a means to an end. Originally the Beastie Boys' own music was supposed to contain SCP-2570 - I replaced that with Kanye's music because A) I thought using Kanye livened up the article with the juxtaposition between rap music and paranormal activity, and B) I liked Decibelles' challenge idea. I did not know about Kanye and the Beastie Boys' history. I used Kanye liberally while discussing the article because again, the juxtaposition makes it more interesting.

As for why Kanye's voice works within the context of the article, my personal head canon is that Ahseem is a lesser Elder God who must follow certain ancient laws. Laws that, among other things, forbid these lesser Gods from intruding upon the territory of even more terrible and powerful Gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Second, what has Kanye got to do with this? The ghouls dislike Kanye in general more than any specific, perhaps mathematically identified, audio? Just all the music by Kanye West happens to be the best for this application? Out of all the sounds that are possible? Kanye West? If you are going to use a living person like this it has got to make sense, maybe I'm missing somethinhg, did this guy really hate Kanye in real life or something? Googling around it looks like Kanye sued the Beastie Boys right when Yauch died, so is it exclusively Kanye's sick beats that neutralize this SCP?

Kanye West has appeared as a cameo in a Beastie Boys video, as well as having sampled them… which did not, in fact, result in Kanye suing the Beastie Boys. It did indeed result in Tuf America suing him, and Tuf America also suing the Beastie Boys on the eve of Yauch's death. I'm pretty sure West and the Beastie Boys were on good terms their whole life, so it's not like, in-universe, it's completely random, nor is it a joke. It'd be more random if Chopin's music were the one able to stop the monsters from coming through the portal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: RB4 releases later this year.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed and even found this a bit funny, which is why I feel this would work much better as -J than a normal article. It feels to out of place as it is now. Its like you took a well known joke/trend of hating on Kanye West and how is music is so bad that even ghosts/entities from another diminesion hate his music and made it into a SCP. I get it, but with an idea that is a joke it just feels like it should be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This whole idea is good, but vague. I think there should be a little bit more to add on because I feel there isn't enough information. Apart from that..

Ghosts that hate Kanye West? That is where you earned your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
neutral vote, three main issues.

First, the actual dialogue in the interview log sounds hollow to me. A (what I consider) mistake a lot of people make, and which you have certainly fallen victim to here, is giving all speaking characters the same way of talking, all the same little quarks and tempos that are normally different for each person. That just flattens the dialogue right out, and it white washes into feeling like one gigantic monologue.

Second, what has Kanye got to do with this? The ghouls dislike Kanye in general more than any specific, perhaps mathematically identified, audio? Just all the music by Kanye West happens to be the best for this application? Out of all the sounds that are possible? Kanye West? If you are going to use a living person like this it has got to make sense, maybe I'm missing somethinhg, did this guy really hate Kanye in real life or something? Googling around it looks like Kanye sued the Beastie Boys right when Yauch died, so is it exclusively Kanye's sick beats that neutralize this SCP?

Third, there is no way this article would be interesting if you didn't call out Kanye West as an involved individual. Now that is kind of a stupid argument (Oh if you took out the interesting part of the SCP it isn't interesting? You don't say…) but I think it gets a little closer to why felt the need to post about how I didn't vote, when you are relying on a gimmick (wacky things involving Kanye West) the gimmick needs to make sense, not seem like it is chucked in for the sake of being a gimmick.

Maybe there is context here I am not aware of, but even then, exactly how far are the readers expected to go to meet you with this SCP? Knowing the nuances of a minor fued (which I'm only speculating exists, based on this article) between one major star and one guy from some past-their-prime band is a decent bit to require of the audience before they can even step in and appreciate the work.

So I feel I have no option by the no-vote, because I'm not aware of if this is even referencing something, let alone what that something is about. This could have been a +1 if the explanation was worked in (although people in the know might find that too on the nose, so, give and take I guess)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! I like it a lot. I think you've simplified it a bit from the draft and amped up the humor with the musical mix-ups, which is great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel a lot of different ways about this, but keeping paranormal beasites at bay with Kanye West grants this article a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honestly not comfortable with skips overtly using real people, living or recently dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do. So much.

I love this skip. It actually uses Kanye West in a great and sensible way, I like the idea of MCA being a skip and how it ties into people who just want to hear him keep making music, and the interview certainly sold me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, Jeltz, and Douglas Fook. Those are some really heavy HGttG references.

Good skip, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I got the HGttG reference, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People still play Rock Band?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made a small edit, insomuch as I'm not aware of anybody named "Kenny Roger".

In other news, all I know about the Beastie Boys is that I can use "Sabotage" to make my Rock Bandmates fall down laughing if I'm on vocals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even know how to react to this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tricia and Arthur Prefect?

Death's too good for you.

item.title: Only Kanye can save us now
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Rushington, MadCatUSA, NomadMonad, Petrograd, TwistedGears, Azzleflux, and Decibelles for critique. Thanks to Azzleflux for the change log and Decibelles for the idea to use Kanye and the title of the article.

See more of my work on my author page!
